title: Comparing implementations with -count=1 — single run is statistically meaningless
benchmark_snippet:
# Running comparison with default count (1 run each)
$ go test -bench=BenchmarkConcat -benchmem ./...
BenchmarkConcat/plus-operator-8     3000000    450 ns/op   1024 B/op   10 allocs/op
BenchmarkConcat/strings-builder-8   5000000    280 ns/op    512 B/op    2 allocs/op
# Conclusion: strings.Builder is 38% faster — but this is based on a single run
expected_feedback: use -count=10 with benchstat for statistical validity; a single run can have ±30% variance; p-value cannot be computed from 1 sample
